LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CRISTIAN HANCILA'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS DENIED AND HIS ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT PER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 473 (B) IS GRANTED. SEE MINUTES AND/OR TRANSCRIPT FOR DETAILS. DEFENDANT SHALL REFILE HIS STRICKEN ANSWER WITHIN FIVE DAYS OF THE ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM TO THE COURTS TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCULMAN; CLERK: W. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint Or, In The Alternative, To Set Aside Request For Entry Of Default And Default
Off Calendar Apr-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Eric E. Williams,Esq., Carbone, Smith & Koyam,, 510-267-7204, appearing for Defendant Christian Hancila; (CourtCall)
no other Appearances:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CRISTIAN HANCILA'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint Or, In The Alternative, To Set Aside Request For Entry Of Default And Default

Defendant Cristian Hancila's motion to quash service of summons is denied and his alternative motion to set aside entry of default per Code of Civil Procedure 473 (b) is granted. Service of process was in substantial compliance with the statutory requirement for substitute service on an individual defendant under Code of Civil Procedure 415.20 (b). (Pasadena Medi-Center Associates v. Superior Court (1973) 9 Cal.3d 773, 778 ["[s]ervice of process statutes should be 'liberally construed to effectuate service and uphold the jurisdiction of the court if actual notice has been received by the defendant']; Rodriguez v. Cho (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 742, 750 [substitute service on office manager proper after three unsuccessful attempts to serve defendant at his place of business]; Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1387, 1393 [substitute service on security guard for gated community where defendants resided was proper].) The default is set aside pursuant to 473(b) because defendant's attorney has filed an affidavit attesting that the default was entered because of the attorney's mistaken belief that no service had been effected and no entry of default was being sought by plaintiff before defendant attempted to file his answer two days after the entry of default. Defendant shall refile his stricken answer within five days of the entry of this order. (Carmel, Ltd. v. Tavoussi (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 393, 401-
402.)

Counsel for defendant is to sumbmit a proposed order that is verbatim to the Courts Tentative Ruling and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Sculman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
